title: The Boron Letters
subtitle: A series of letters on getting rich in direct marketing — and living a good life
author: Gary C. Halbert
writtenPeriod: 1990–1992 (originally published in a free newsletter before being compiled into book form)

recommendedEdition.title: The Boron Letters
recommendedEdition.note: The definitive compilation. The letters were originally distributed for free via the Gary Halbert newsletter in the early 1990s. This edition collects all 17 letters with an introduction by Bond Halbert, who assisted his father's work. The Boron 2.0 edition by Bond Halbert (2024) provides updated commentary and expanded context.

themes: Start with a hot market, then find a product
The AIDA formula (Attention, Interest, Desire, Action)
Why to quit good jobs to do your own thing
Copy longhand to learn great writing
Persistence in the face of failure
Ethical persuasion and honesty in selling
excerpt: The Boron Letters are a collection of 17 informal, deeply personal letters written by legendary direct-response copywriter Gary C. Halbert to his youngest son Bond while Gary was incarcerated in a minimum-security federal prison in Boron, California in 1990. Far more than a copywriting manual, the letters are a father's transmitted wisdom: how to think about markets, how to write irresistible sales copy, why the most important marketing decision is choosing the right product for the right audience, and how to live with discipline, curiosity, and integrity. The '2-letter month' — the idea that if you learn to write two letters really well, your success is virtually guaranteed — remains one of the most cited frameworks in direct-response marketing. The letters blend hard business technique with Stoic-flavored life philosophy: avoid HALT decisions, copy great writing longhand, never quit what you're doing without something better in hand, and always begin with a hot market.
